ieo_ui.name: Upwelling index costero del IEO.
ieo_ui.description: Provided by the Spanish Institute of Oceanography (IEO), this dataset focuses on the Coastal Upwelling Index. The index supports the study and monitoring of upwelling events, which significantly affect biological productivity and marine biodiversity in coastal areas.

ieo_capturas.name: Datos de abundancias y distribución de especies.
ieo_capturas.description: Provided by the Spanish Institute of Oceanography (IEO) in collaboration with PESCASOST, this dataset contains species abundance and distribution data from the IEO DEMERSALES surveys. It reports the presence and abundance of species in the areas covered by the surveys.

ieo_fitoplancton_flowcam.name: Imágenes de fitoplancton con Flowcam
ieo_fitoplancton_flowcam.description: This dataset contains phytoplankton images captured with the FlowCam system and subsequently processed using the EcoTaxa platform. The images were classified, labelled and organized in EcoTaxa to provide a standardized, high-resolution resource for ecological and oceanographic analysis of phytoplankton diversity and abundance.

ccmm_ieo.datos_descripcion:
Data type: Multidisciplinary oceanographic data series and numerical model results of the marine ecosystem. Data source: Sustained ocean observations and numerical models generated and maintained by IEO-CSIC. Intended use of the data: Analysis of oceanographic environmental time series; development of applications and services for end users; integration with other datasets in the data space.

Oceanographic data available in the data space. Standard data and service catalogs integrated into the OneHealth DataSpace ecosystem.

ieo_carabelas_drift_risk.name: Calculation of the probability of Portuguese man o' war appearing on Galician beaches
ieo_carabelas_drift_risk.description: Operational system that simulates the drift of Portuguese man o' war and calculates the probability of their arrival at beaches in Galicia.
ieo_carabelas_drift_risk.descripcion_ampliada:
This use case runs daily drift simulations with OpenDrift using thousands of virtual particles that represent possible Portuguese man o' war off the Galician coast. The system combines ocean currents and wind fields, generates individual trajectories and post-processes the results to estimate the probability of arrival at each beach.

The simulations use currents from Copernicus Marine and INTECMAR's HF Radar, together with wind data from MeteoGalicia. The alert level for each beach is calculated as the percentage of particles reaching its vicinity in the worst-case scenario over the following four days. It is a probabilistic estimate, not a certainty. The process integrates simulation, post-processing, PostgreSQL storage and visualisation through a web application and Grafana.
ieo_carabelas_drift_risk.volumen: Approximately 11 GB in the historical sample
ieo_carabelas_drift_risk.n_ficheros: 2 historical CSV files; continuous operational generation

ieo_carabelas_drift_risk.datos_descripcion:
Data type: Particle trajectories generated with OpenDrift and aggregated beach-risk results. Data source: Simulations using oceanographic model data, MeteoGalicia winds and INTECMAR HF Radar currents for reanalysis. Intended use of the data: Drift prediction and analysis, identification of exposed coastal areas and estimation of the risk of particles reaching beaches.

The shared historical sample contains approximately 10 GB of trajectories and 1 GB of beach-level processed results. The operational service continuously generates new data.

cetmar_cortegada_hydrophone.name: Cortegada Hydrophone — Underwater noise monitoring
cetmar_cortegada_hydrophone.description: Underwater acoustic data recorded by the hydrophone installed at Cortegada to study marine noise and its main sources.
cetmar_cortegada_hydrophone.descripcion_ampliada:
This use case brings together data provided by the Cortegada hydrophone, installed on an Ocean-Meteorological Observation Network platform located by the island. The recordings enable monitoring of the underwater soundscape and identification of the main noise sources in this area of the Rías Baixas.

The monitoring forms part of CETMAR's work to improve knowledge of marine noise and support more sustainable fishing and shellfishing activities with a lower acoustic impact.

hydrophone_recordings.name: hidrofono_cortegada_cetmar
hydrophone_recordings.description: Recordings and associated data provided by the Cortegada hydrophone.

cetmar_cortegada_hydrophone.datos_descripcion: Data type: Underwater acoustic recordings in WAV format and associated data in CSV format. Data source: Hydrophone installed at Cortegada and managed as part of CETMAR's marine monitoring activities. Intended use of the data: Study of the underwater soundscape, identification of noise sources and analysis of their impact on the marine environment.

depourense.name: Publication of Telecare Data
depourense.description: The Diputación Provincial de Ourense publishes in OneHealth DataSpace four datasets from its home telecare service, with anonymized or pseudonymized information on follow-ups, device malfunctions, beneficiary characteristics and emergency events.
depourense.descripcion_ampliada:
The Deputación Provincial de Ourense operates a home telecare service for older people and people requiring assistance. By joining OneHealth DataSpace as a data provider, it makes four datasets derived from the service's operations available to the ecosystem. These cover the main aspects of care: follow-up contacts, technical device incidents, beneficiaries' operational profiles and emergency events. The data are published in pseudonymized form to protect users' privacy.

Real, structured data from public telecare services are scarce. The Deputación de Ourense has identified an opportunity to contribute information to the health and dependency data ecosystem that can support improvements to similar services, research into dependency care and the development of tools for analysing older people's well-being.
depourense.datos_descripcion:
Data type: Four datasets published in OneHealth DataSpace. (1) dataset-seguimiento: follow-up contacts from the telecare service (scheduled, proactive, or reactive), with outcomes, reasons, and relative frequency compared to historical data. (2) dataset-averias: technical incidents and device breakdowns in telecare devices (device type, breakdown type, opening and resolution dates, final status). (3) dataset-beneficiarios: pseudo-anonymised information on service users (non-sensitive attributes: service status, registration/deregistration dates, and operational characteristics). (4) dataset-emergencias: events classified as emergencies (typology, priority, timestamps, originating device, and outcome of the intervention). Data source: Management system of the home telecare service of the Provincial Council of Ourense. Intended use of the data: Analysis of usage patterns in public telecare services; research into dependency care and active ageing; development of tools for improving care and early detection of risk situations.

Four public telecare datasets available in OneHealth DataSpace, covering the main operational aspects of the service: follow-up, breakdowns, beneficiaries, and emergencies. Contribution of local public administration to the health and dependency data ecosystem. Reference for other telecare services wishing to share similar data.

ccvet.name: CcVet — Digitization of Veterinary Clinical Cases for Data Spaces
ccvet.description: CcVet aims to digitize all information surrounding veterinary clinical cases so that it can be used in data spaces to cross-reference it with other related information, especially that related to human and environmental health. The project seeks.
ccvet.descripcion_ampliada:
CcVet aims to digitize all information surrounding veterinary clinical cases so that it can be used in data spaces to cross-reference it with other related information, especially that related to human and environmental health. The project seeks to structure and standardize veterinary clinical practice data to make it interoperable with other health information systems within the OneHealth framework.

Veterinary prescribing is subject to rigorous controls based on current regulations, especially RD 666/2023, which is based on the premise that clinical case data is perfectly documented. However, this information is dispersed and not available in interoperable formats that allow cross-analysis with human and environmental health data.
ccvet.datos_descripcion:
Data type: Disease classification dataset according to WHO standards integrated with new official and unofficial classifications. Approximately 1,000 records in CSV format. Data source: WOAH (World Organisation for Animal Health) and other official classification sources. Intended use of the data: Cross-analysis with human and environmental health data; study of zoonotic diseases and their cross-cutting OneHealth impact; validation of veterinary prescriptions under RD 666/2023.

Structured veterinary clinical case data available in the data space for cross-analysis with human and environmental health information. Improved traceability in veterinary prescription and support for the study of zoonotic diseases.

Structured veterinary clinical case dataset available in the data space. Demonstration at the project's final event.

two.name: Integration of Environmental and Agronomic Data in the OneHealth DataSpace
two.description: Integration and sharing of TRUEWORLD environmental and agronomic data in OneHealth DataSpace. The use case incorporates environmental monitoring data collected by weather stations and IoT sensors (temperature, humidity, precipitation, wind, solar radiation) together with agronomic data (evapotranspiration, vegetation index, soil moisture).
two.descripcion_ampliada:
Integration and sharing of TRUEWORLD environmental and agronomic data in OneHealth DataSpace. The use case incorporates environmental monitoring data collected by weather stations and IoT sensors (temperature, humidity, precipitation, wind, solar radiation) together with agronomic data (evapotranspiration, vegetation index, soil moisture). The pilot dataset covers data from the Balearic Islands.

Need to structure and share the data generated by TRUEWORLD's sensor network and monitoring systems to maximize their value and reuse in the OneHealth data ecosystem. Distributed environmental and agronomic data have high potential for environmental health applications and climate risk management, but lack an interoperable sharing channel.
two.datos_descripcion:
Data type: Meteorological and agronomic data from Valldemossa (Balearic Islands). Meteorological parameters: air temperature (max./min.), relative humidity, dew point, wet-bulb temperature, wind speed and direction, maximum gust, thermal sensation, heat index, accumulated and intensity rainfall, solar radiation, evapotranspiration, UV index, heating and cooling degree days. Agronomic parameters: soil temperature, soil moisture, leaf moisture (max., min., and accumulated time). ID of the asset published in OneHealth DataSpace: meteo-dataset-v3. Data source: TRUEWORLD meteorological station and agricultural IoT sensors in Valldemossa, Balearic Islands. Periodic continuous monitoring records. Intended use of the data: Environmental and agronomic monitoring; health and environmental impact analysis; precision agriculture; climate risk management and adaptation to climate change.

Environmental and agronomic data available in the OneHealth DataSpace data space, with applications in precision agriculture, environmental monitoring, climate risk management, and analysis of environmental impact on human health.

Environmental and agronomic data dataset (meteo-dataset-v3) available in the OneHealth DataSpace data space. Replicable model for integrating IoT sensor data into health data ecosystems.

evo2_viral_genomes.name: Evo 2 — Fine-tuning with viral genomes
evo2_viral_genomes.description: Fine-tuning of Evo 2 models of different sizes with eukaryotic-host and broad-host-range viral genomes using LoRA.
evo2_viral_genomes.descripcion_ampliada: This use case will fine-tune Evo 2 models of different sizes with eukaryotic-host and broad-host-range viral genome data using LoRA.
evo2_viral_genomes.datos_descripcion:
Data type: Eukaryotic-host and broad-host-range viral genomes prepared for fine-tuning Evo 2 with LoRA. Intended use of the data: Fine-tuning Evo 2 models of different sizes and evaluating their adaptation to viral genomic sequences.

Evo 2 models of different sizes fine-tuned with the selected viral genome dataset using LoRA.

intecmar_radar_hf.name: Galicia HF Radar — Hourly surface-current data
intecmar_radar_hf.dataset_name: Hourly data from the Galicia HF Radar
intecmar_radar_hf.description: Hourly surface-current data estimated by the Galicia HF Radar Network and processed in near real time on a 6 km resolution grid.
intecmar_radar_hf.descripcion_ampliada:
Current velocities estimated by HF radar represent approximately the upper 0.3–2.5 metres of the ocean. Hourly radial measurements from stations in the Galicia HF Radar Network are combined using unweighted least squares to generate near-real-time surface-current maps on a 6 km grid off the Galician coast.

The dataset supports observation of surface ocean circulation.
intecmar_radar_hf.datos_descripcion:
Data type: Three-dimensional grid of surface ocean-current velocities, with hourly temporal resolution and 6 km spatial resolution. Distributed as NetCDF 4.3.3 using reference system EPSG:3041. Data source: Radial velocity measurements from the Galicia HF Radar Network, operated by INTECMAR with the participation of Puertos del Estado. Intended use of the data: Visualisation and analysis of surface currents off the Galician coast, using the best product available at processing time.

The source catalogue states that there are no general use limitations, but also indicates that Puertos del Estado must be contacted to request permission to use the data.
intecmar_radar_hf.entidad_promotora: Technological Institute for the Control of the Marine Environment of Galicia (INTECMAR)

intecmar_upwelling_index.name: Coastal upwelling index for Galicia and northern Portugal
intecmar_upwelling_index.description: Data used to calculate and map the coastal upwelling index along Galicia and northern Portugal.
intecmar_upwelling_index.descripcion_ampliada:
This use case brings together the data used to calculate the upwelling index and the resulting maps for the coast of Galicia and northern Portugal. The index supports analysis of coastal circulation dynamics and their relationship with biological productivity and marine biodiversity.

The entry recovers two datasets from the legacy Marine Sciences project: the parameters used in the calculation and the mapped upwelling-index product.

intecmar_upwelling_index.datos_descripcion:
Data type: Station parameters and sea-current velocity components used in the calculation, together with coastal upwelling-index maps. Legacy format: Parquet. Data source: INTECMAR and the legacy Marine Sciences project catalogue. Intended use of the data: Calculation, visualisation and analysis of coastal upwelling in Galicia and northern Portugal.

The legacy source does not specify volume, number of files, licence, temporal coverage or temporal resolution.
